content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown

119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`

122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Genus-`0` base objects: `ℙ¹`, `Ga`, `Gm`, and the `Gm`-scaling action `σ_×`
10	
11	This file ships the concrete genus-`0` base-case infrastructure (iter-165 lane) that
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity.morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` consumes via
13	the **`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut** (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Proposition 3.10): the total
14	scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m → ℙ¹`, `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, feeds the proven Cor 1.5
15	(`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`); the `W`-axis collapses at the scaling fixed point
16	`0 ∈ ℙ¹`, giving `f(λx) = f(x)`; density of `𝔾_m ⊆ ℙ¹` plus `A` separated
17	(`ext_of_eqOnOpen`) force `f` constant. NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO
18	`Hom(𝔾_a, A) = 0`, char-general. See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`
19	(`def:genus0_base_objects`, `def:gaTranslationP1`).
20	
21	The file is **upstream** of `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean` (the mathlib-analogist
22	`gm-scaling-p1` D4 verdict: split into a focused file per scheme construction, mirroring
23	the precedent of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.{Smooth,Abelian}`). The AVR.lean refactor +
24	proof of `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` is iter-166's lane and does NOT happen here.
25	
26	## The four declarations
27	
28	1. `ProjectiveLineBar` — the projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄`, encoded as `Proj 𝒜` of
29	   the standard ℕ-graded `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`, viewed as an object of
30	   `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`. `IsProper` is FREE from
31	   `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` (the algebra is finite type).
32	   `GeometricallyIrreducible` and `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` are project-side sub-builds
33	   (Mathlib does not ship these for `Proj`), left as scaffold `sorry`s for iter-166+.
34	
35	2. `Ga` — the additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄`, encoded as
36	   `(AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).asOver _`. `IsAffine` is FREE; `GrpObj` is
37	   installed via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with the additive-group functor
38	   `T ↦ AddGrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)`; `Smooth` is FREE from
39	   `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed`.
40	
41	3. `Gm` — the multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄`, encoded as
42	   `(Spec (.of (Localization.Away (X : MvPolynomial Unit k̄)))).asOver _`
43	   (= `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`, AFFINE — NOT the basic-open path). `IsAffine` is FREE; `GrpObj` is
44	   installed via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with the units functor
45	   `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`; `Smooth` is FREE from
46	   `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed`.
47	
48	4. `gmScalingP1 : ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar` — the bare scaling
49	   morphism `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, with companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposing
50	   the load-bearing fixed-point property at `0 ∈ ℙ¹` that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`
51	   (Cor 1.5) needs.
52	
53	The blueprint chapter `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` covers this file via
54	`% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean
55	AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean`.
56	-/
57	
58	set_option autoImplicit false
59	set_option linter.style.setOption false
60	
61	universe u
62	
63	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
64	
65	noncomputable section
66	
67	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
68	
69	/-! ### (A) The projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄` (the `Proj` realisation) -/
70	
71	/-- The **standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`** by total degree.
72	
73	This is the homogeneous-component decomposition `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule`
74	specialised to two variables (`Fin 2`) over `k̄`. The `GradedRing` instance is
75	`MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` (free).
76	
77	Used to define `ProjectiveLineBar = Proj` of this graded ring. -/
78	abbrev projectiveLineBarGrading (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
79	    ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
80	  MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar
81	
82	instance projectiveLineBarGrading_gradedRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
83	    GradedRing (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) :=
84	  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra
85	
86	/-- **The projective line over `Spec k̄` as a scheme.** This is `Proj 𝒜` of the standard
87	ℕ-graded `k̄[X₀, X₁]`. Carries an `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance via
88	`Proj.toSpecZero` composed with the algebra-map identification of the degree-`0` piece
89	with `k̄` (Mathlib's `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebraSubtypeMemOfNat`).
90	
91	`IsProper` is FREE — see the `instIsProper` instance below. `IsAlgClosed kbar` is *not*
92	required for the scheme itself but is needed for downstream `Smooth`/geometric
93	irreducibility instances. -/
94	def ProjectiveLineBarScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
95	  Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
96	
97	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` structure on `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` via
98	`Proj.toSpecZero` and the identification `k̄ ≃ ↥(𝒜 0)`. -/
99	instance projectiveLineBarScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
100	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
101	  hom := Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
102	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
103	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))
104	
105	/-- **The projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` as an object of `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** This is the
106	concrete scheme used by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (iter-166) to formalise the
107	`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut. -/
108	def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
109	  (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
110	
111	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
112	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
113	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
114	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
115	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
116	
117	The chain:
118	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
119	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
120	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
121	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
122	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
123	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
124	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
125	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
126	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
127	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
128	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
129	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
130	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
131	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
132	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))
133	  -- The IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ kbar) needed for the FiniteType derivation.
134	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar
135	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
136	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
137	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
138	  -- MvPolynomial.{Fin 2} k̄ is finite type over ↥(𝒜 0) (chained from finite-type over k̄).
139	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType
140	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
141	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
142	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _
143	  -- The algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective: the degree-`0` piece of the homogeneous
144	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity
145	  -- follows from `MvPolynomial.C_injective`. Surjectivity uses
146	  -- `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem` (which says the degree-`0` component of a
147	  -- homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial is itself) plus `homogeneousComponent_zero`
148	  -- (which expresses the degree-`0` component as `C` of the constant coefficient).
149	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar
150	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)) := by
151	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
152	    · intro x y h
153	      apply MvPolynomial.C_injective (Fin 2) kbar
154	      exact congrArg Subtype.val h
155	    · intro ⟨v, hv⟩
156	      refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
157	      apply Subtype.ext
158	      rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
159	      have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
160	      -- `key : (homogeneousComponent 0) v = if 0 = 0 then v else 0` which simp resolves
161	      -- and combines with `homogeneousComponent_zero` to give `C (coeff 0 v) = v`.
162	      simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
163	      exact key
164	  -- Therefore `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is iso, and composition of proper + iso is
165	  -- proper.
166	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
167	      (algebraMap kbar ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)))) := by
168	    rw [isIso_SpecMap_iff]
169	    exact hbij
170	  infer_instance
171	
172	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold sorry
173	(Mathlib does not ship `GeometricallyIrreducible` for `Proj` of a polynomial ring;
174	plan-marked acceptable for iter-165). -/
175	instance projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
176	    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
177	  sorry
178	
179	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is smooth of relative dimension `1` over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold
180	sorry (Mathlib does not ship `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` for `Proj`; plan-marked
181	acceptable for iter-165). -/
182	instance projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
183	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
184	  sorry
185	
186	/-! ### The standard `k̄`-points `0`, `1`, `∞` on `ℙ¹`
187	
188	The three distinguished `k̄`-points of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` are `[0 : 1]` (the affine origin), `[1 : 1]`
189	(the affine unit), and `[1 : 0]` (the point at infinity). They are encoded as morphisms
190	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, i.e. sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`.
191	
192	The construction goes through `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`: a `k̄`-point of `Proj 𝒜` is
193	specified by an evaluation ring map `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* k̄` (composed into
194	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) whose image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)`
195	generates the unit ideal — automatic whenever the evaluation vector has a unit coordinate. -/
196	
197	/-- **The underlying ring map of a `k̄`-point of `ℙ¹`.** Sends `X₀ ↦ v 0`, `X₁ ↦ v 1`,
198	then composes back into `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
199	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
200	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) :
201	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) :=
202	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.eval v)
203	
204	/-- **Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top condition** for `ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal`: if one
205	of the coordinates `v 0` or `v 1` is a unit, then the image of `(X₀, X₁)` generates the
206	unit ideal in `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`. -/
207	private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top
208	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
209	    Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
210	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤ := by
211	  have hX_mem : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
212	      (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
213	    HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one
214	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
215	  have hImg_mem : ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i) ∈
216	      Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
217	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
218	    Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hX_mem
219	  have hImg_unit : IsUnit (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i)) := by
220	    unfold ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
221	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval_X]
222	    exact hi.map _
223	  rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
224	  obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hImg_unit
225	  rw [show (1 : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)) = ((u⁻¹ : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)ˣ) : _) * u from
226	    (Units.inv_mul _).symm]
227	  exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hu.symm ▸ hImg_mem)
228	
229	/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
230	`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
231	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
232	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
233	`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
234	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
235	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
236	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
237	  Over.homMk
238	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
239	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
240	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
241	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
242	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
243	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
244	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
245	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
246	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
247	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
248	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
249	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
250	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
251	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
252	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
253	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
254	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
255	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
256	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
257	      ext r
258	      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
259	          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
260	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
261	    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
262	            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
263	            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
264	      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _
265	
266	/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
267	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
268	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
269	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
270	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)
271	
272	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 = [1 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
273	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
274	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
275	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
276	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)
277	
278	/-- The `k̄`-point `∞ = [1 : 0] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
279	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 0`. -/
280	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
281	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
282	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)
283	
284	/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/
285	
286	/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
287	affine line `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
288	presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
289	def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
290	  AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))
291	
292	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GaScheme` (via
293	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.over`). -/
294	instance gaScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
295	    (GaScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
296	  inferInstanceAs ((AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
297	
298	/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
299	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
300	abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
301	  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
302	
303	/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
304	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
305	instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
306	    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
307	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
308	    (Spec (.of kbar))))
309	
310	/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
311	`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
312	instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
313	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
314	  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
315	    (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))
316	
317	/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
318	`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
319	transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
320	`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/
321	instance ga_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Ga kbar).left :=
322	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine (Fin 1) _).hom
323	
324	/-- **`GrpObj`-structure on `𝔾_a` via `ofRepresentableBy`.**
325	
326	Installs the additive-group structure on `Ga` using the Yoneda installer
327	`GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` (Mathlib's canonical mechanism) with the additive-group functor
328	`T ↦ AddGrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)` and the representable-by witness derived from
329	`AffineSpace.homOverEquiv`. Scaffold: full body is iter-166's lane (the homEquiv +
330	naturality glue is straightforward but needs careful unfolding).
331	
332	Scaffold body for iter-166+; off-path for the genus-0 closure (the rigidity consumer
333	`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` uses `W = Gm`, not `W = Ga` — `Ga` is on the demoted
334	additive route only). -/
335	instance ga_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Ga kbar) := sorry
336	
337	/-- **`𝔾_a` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
338	`AlgebraicGeometry.smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once `GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`,
339	and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
340	instance ga_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
341	    Smooth (Ga kbar).hom :=
342	  have : GrpObj (Over.mk (Ga kbar).hom) := ga_grpObj kbar
343	  smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed (Ga kbar).hom
344	
345	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
346	
347	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
348	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
349	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
350	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
351	
352	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
353	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
354	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
355	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
356	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
357	
358	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
359	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
360	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
361	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
362	
363	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
364	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
365	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
366	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
367	
368	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
369	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
370	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
371	
372	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
373	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
374	element is finitely presented), bridged through
375	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
376	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
377	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
378	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
379	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
380	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
381	      inferInstance)
382	
383	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
384	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
385	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
386	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
387	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
388	
389	/-- **`GrpObj`-structure on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`.**
390	
391	Installs the multiplicative-group structure on `Gm` using `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with
392	the units functor `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. The representable-by witness exploits the
393	fact that morphisms into `Spec (Localization.Away t)` correspond exactly to units in the
394	global sections (Mathlib's `IsLocalization.Away`-Spec bijection). Scaffold body — same
395	discipline as `ga_grpObj`.
396	
397	This `GrpObj Gm` is the LIVE consumer of the iter-166 `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`
398	proof body (the `𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut applies `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` with
399	`W = Gm`). -/
400	instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) := sorry
401	
402	/-- **`𝔾_m` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once
403	`GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`, and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
404	instance gm_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
405	    Smooth (Gm kbar).hom :=
406	  have : GrpObj (Over.mk (Gm kbar).hom) := gm_grpObj kbar
407	  smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed (Gm kbar).hom
408	
409	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 ∈ 𝔾_m` (the multiplicative identity), encoded as the group-object
410	unit `η[Gm kbar] : 𝟙_ ⟶ Gm kbar`. -/
411	def Gm.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
412	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Gm kbar :=
413	  η[Gm kbar]
414	
415	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
416	
417	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
418	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
419	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
420	
421	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
422	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
423	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
424	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
425	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
426	
427	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
428	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
429	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
430	
431	/-- **The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`** in `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.
432	
433	Scaffold body — iter-165 lands the *type signature* (the concrete object the iter-166
434	proof refactor of `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` consumes); the chartwise glue body
435	is iter-166's lane (or an even later sub-build if `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` requires
436	further infrastructure). -/
437	def gmScalingP1 (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
438	    ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⊗ Gm kbar ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
439	  sorry
440	
441	/-- **The load-bearing fixed-point property of `σ_×`:** at the scaling fixed point
442	`0 ∈ ℙ¹`, the morphism `σ_×(0, ·) : 𝔾_m → ℙ¹` is the constant morphism at `0`. That is,
443	the composite `(0 ≫ toUnit) × 𝟙 : 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹` equals `toUnit ≫ 0`.
444	
445	This is precisely the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis `_hf` that
446	`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes when applied with `V = ℙ¹` proper,
447	`W = 𝔾_m`, base points `0 ∈ ℙ¹`, `1 ∈ 𝔾_m`.
448	
449	Scaffold body — iter-165 lands the *statement* (matching the rigidity consumer's `_hf`
450	shape); the proof body is iter-166's lane (it reduces to a chart-level computation: on
451	`𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m`, `(0, λ) ↦ λ·0 = 0` is a defequal ring-map check). -/
452	lemma gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
453	    lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar)) ≫
454	        gmScalingP1 kbar =
455	      toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar := by
456	  sorry
457	
458	end AlgebraicGeometry
459	
460	end

1	\chapter{Abelian-variety rigidity: morphisms from a genus-$0$ curve into an abelian variety are constant}
2	\label{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean
4	
5	% Local macro: diagrammatic (Lean `≫`) composition. Defined locally because this
6	% chapter is the writer's only editable file; flagged in the writer report for promotion
7	% to macros/common.tex if desired. \providecommand is a no-op if it is later added globally.
8	\providecommand{\fatsemi}{\mathbin{\,;\,}}
9	
10	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-157). This chapter is the dedicated home of the project's COMMITTED
11	% genus-0 route (route (c)): the characteristic-free abelian-variety rigidity stack. It is
12	% 1:1 with the new upstream Lean file AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean, which
13	% imports AlgebraicJacobian.Genus and is imported by AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian (breaking the
14	% RigidityKbar -> Rigidity -> Jacobian import cycle so that genusZeroWitness can consume the
15	% keystone directly). The headline thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV is the char-free
16	% replacement (no [CharZero]) for the over-k-bar declaration rigidity_over_kbar of
17	% \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} (which remains in tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and
18	% still carries [CharZero]). The df=0 / H^0(P^1,Omega)=0 / Serre-duality framing is the
19	% fallback (a), NOT the route developed here.
20	
21	The genus-$0$ arm of the Jacobian-witness construction has a \emph{trivial} witness object
22	($J = \Spec k$); its only remaining mathematical content is a rigidity \emph{statement}:
23	every pointed morphism from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve of genus $0$
24	into an abelian variety is the constant morphism at the identity. We prove this
25	\textbf{characteristic-free} --- the protected goal is over an arbitrary field $k$, so the
26	cheaper differential argument (via $H^0(\mathbb P^1, \Omega) = H^0(\mathbb P^1, \mathcal
27	O(-2)) = 0$, hence $df = 0$, hence $f$ constant) is unavailable: it breaks on a
28	characteristic-$p$ Frobenius-descent wall, where a nonconstant $f$ can have $df = 0$. The
29	rigidity route below sidesteps that wall.
30	
31	The chain has two parts. The \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford,
32	Form~I; equivalently Milne's Rigidity Theorem~1.1) is an elementary properness/closed-map
33	argument --- it uses neither the theorem of the cube nor any cohomology, is the entry point
34	for formalisation, and is now proven axiom-clean. From the Rigidity Lemma, Milne's \S I.3
35	derives the genus-$0$ base case \emph{without} the theorem of the cube --- and, in this
36	project's reading, \emph{without} Milne's Theorem~3.2 (rational-map extension), Lemma~3.3,
37	Auslander--Buchsbaum, or differentials (the ``4th route''; see
38	\cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}). The decisive observation is that $\mathbb P^1$ is
39	\emph{already complete} and $f \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A$ is \emph{already a total morphism}, so
40	there is no rational defect map to extend over a complete surface and Theorem~3.2 never enters.
41	Instead the base case is closed by the \textbf{$\mathbb G_m$-scaling shortcut} (the project's
42	primary route, an Archon-original realisation of Milne Proposition~3.9): one uses the
43	\emph{multiplicative scaling} action $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to
44	\mathbb P^1$, $(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x$ (the M\"obius scaling fixing both $0$ and
45	$\infty$), which is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb
46	G_m$ (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1}). Normalising $f(0) = \eta_A$ and composing $h := \sigma_\times
47	\fatsemi f$, one applies additivity of $\mathrm{Hom}(-, A)$ over a product
48	(\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}, Milne Corollary~1.5) --- whose Lean signature requires
49	only the \emph{first} factor complete --- with $V = \mathbb P^1$ proper, $W = \mathbb G_m$, and
50	base points $0 \in \mathbb P^1$, $1 \in \mathbb G_m$. Because $0$ is a \emph{fixed point} of
51	scaling, the $W$-axis restriction $\lambda \mapsto h(0, \lambda) = f(\lambda \cdot 0) = f(0)$ is
52	\emph{constant} at $\eta_A$, so the decomposition collapses to $h = p \fatsemi f$, i.e.\
53	$f(\lambda x) = f(x)$. Specialising $x = 1$ gives that $f|_{\mathbb G_m}$ is constant, and since
54	$\mathbb G_m$ is dense in $\mathbb P^1$ with $A$ separated, $f$ is constant
55	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, via the separated-target/dominant-source handle
56	\cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}). This route uses \emph{only} the proven Corollary~1.5, a
57	scaling fixed point, and density --- it never forms the additive defect map, never invokes
58	$\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$, and so sidesteps the ``image of a group homomorphism is a
59	closed subgroup'' input that has no Mathlib support. The classical $\mathbb G_a$-additive
60	argument (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial} via the $\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m$ incompatibility
61	\cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}, the heart of Milne Proposition~3.9) is retained only as the
62	Milne-faithful alternative and is \emph{off} the genus-$0$ critical path. The corollary that a
63	pointed regular map of abelian
64	varieties is a homomorphism (\cref{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom}, Milne Corollary~1.2) is recorded
65	for completeness and for Route~A but is \emph{not} needed on the genus-$0$ path; Milne's
66	Theorem~3.2 (\cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}) is likewise demoted to a Route-A-only item
67	(the Albanese universal property), no longer on the genus-$0$ critical path
68	(\cref{rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap}). Combining these
69	gives that every morphism $\mathbb P^1 \to A$ is constant
70	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); composing with the classification of genus-$0$
71	curves (\cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}, $C \cong \mathbb P^1$ over $\bar k$) yields the
72	headline \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. The theorem of the cube (Milne \S I.5) is
73	\emph{not} used anywhere on this path (see \cref{rmk:cube_not_needed}); the chain invokes
74	neither Serre duality nor the FGA $\Pic$ representability engine, so the genus-$0$ arm is
75	fully decoupled from Route~A.
76	
77	\medskip
78	
79	Throughout, $\bar k$ is an algebraically closed field of arbitrary characteristic. An
80	\emph{abelian variety} over $\bar k$ is a complete (proper) irreducible group variety; in the
81	project's Lean encoding $A$ is an object of $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)$ carrying
82	$\mathtt{GrpObj}$, $\mathtt{IsProper}$, $\mathtt{Smooth}$, and $\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}$.
83	
84	\section{The Rigidity Lemma}
85	
86	\begin{lemma}
87	
88	\leanok
89	[Rigidity Lemma (Mumford, Form I)]
90	  \label{thm:rigidity_lemma}
91	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_lemma}
92	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
93	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "RIGIDITY LEMMA. (Form I.) Let $X$ be a complete variety, $Y$ and $Z$
94	  % any varieties, and $f \colon X \times Y \to Z$ a morphism such that for some
95	  % $y_0 \in Y$, $f(X \times \{y_0\})$ is a single point $z_0$ of $Z$. Then there is a
96	  % morphism $g \colon Y \to Z$ such that if $p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y$ is the
97	  % projection, $f = g \circ p_2$."
98	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43.}
99	  Let $X$ be a complete variety, and let $Y$, $Z$ be any varieties over $\bar k$. Let
100	  $f \colon X \times Y \to Z$ be a morphism such that for some $y_0 \in Y$ the image

50	  ships `Proj.toSpecZero`, `Proj.awayι : Spec (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f) ⟶ Proj 𝒜`

56	  is Mathlib's Yoneda installer for `GrpObj`. Signature:

58	  The 6 uses across Mathlib are the abstract `yonedaGrpObjRepresentableBy` companion

62	  [LocallyOfFinitePresentation f] + [IsReduced G] + [IsAlgClosed K] → Smooth f`.

66	  - `IsLocalization.Away.lift (x : R) (hg : IsUnit (g x)) : S →+* P` (`L471`) —
67	    factor a ring map `R →+* P` through `Localization.Away x` when `x` lands in
68	    the units.

70	  - `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit` (`L82`) — `algebraMap R S x` is a unit.

72	  — `(X ⟶ Spec R) ≃ (R ⟶ Γ(X, ⊤))` (the global-sections / Spec adjunction).

74	  `pullback (Spec.map (algebraMap R S)) (Spec.map (algebraMap R T)) ≅ Spec (S ⊗[R] T)`.
75	  Plus `pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst` / `_snd` — the chart's pullback is computed by an
76	  ordinary ring-tensor.

78	  chartwise morphisms once they agree on pairwise pullbacks. The agreement hypothesis is
79	  `pullback.fst (𝒰.f x) (𝒰.f y) ≫ f x = pullback.snd _ _ ≫ f y` (NOT `f x = f y` on
80	  the chart intersection — it's the proper pullback formulation).

84	  `Spec (Away 𝒜 X₁)`. The companion `affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` (`Basic.lean:324`)

88	  - `GeometricallyIrreducible.comp` (`L121`): `[GI f] [GI g] [UniversallyOpen f]
89	    [UniversallyOpen g] → GI (f ≫ g)`.

91	  [LocallyOfFinitePresentation f] → UniversallyOpen f`. And `[Smooth f] → [Flat f]`
92	  (`Morphisms/Smooth.lean:113`), so `Smooth ⟹ UniversallyOpen` via `Smooth ⟹
93	  LocallyOfFinitePresentation ⟹ Flat + LocallyOfFinitePresentation`.

95	  (`L63`) AND `IsStableUnderBaseChange` (`L79`). So `(X ⊗ Y).hom = pullback.fst X.hom
96	  Y.hom ≫ X.hom` (or `.snd ≫ Y.hom`) is LOFT whenever both factors are.

102	- **Smooth → GeometricallyReduced**: **NOT shipped at scheme level**. (At ring level:
103	  `Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced` exists but is not bridged to `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyReduced`.) This is a real gap for the project.
104	- **`Proj` is reduced of a reduced graded ring**: NOT shipped. Mathlib has neither
105	  `IsReduced (Proj 𝒜)` for `𝒜` over a domain nor `IsIntegral (Proj 𝒜)`. The standard
106	  technique is `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `Proj.affineOpenCover` (each chart is a

110	## Decisions identified
111	
112	### Decision Q1: realization of `Gm` / `Ga`
113	
114	- **Mathlib idiom**: Mathlib has no `𝔾_m` / `𝔾_a` / `GroupScheme.Gm` etc. The closest
115	  existing pieces are (i) `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace` (used by the project for `Ga`),
116	  (ii) `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth` (which CONSUMES an arbitrary `GrpObj`),
117	  and (iii) `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Pullbacks.Scheme.GrpObjAsOverPullback`
118	  (`Pullbacks.lean:808`) which transports a `GrpObj` along a pullback. The project's
119	  encoding `Gm = (Spec (.of (Localization.Away (X : MvPolynomial Unit k̄)))).asOver _`
120	  is the natural "affine `Spec` of `k̄[t, t⁻¹]`" path.
121	- **Project's current path**: as above; landed iter-165 (see `gm-scaling-p1` analogy).
122	- **Gap**: identical-where-Mathlib-has-it, NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL where it doesn't.
123	  The project is NOT building a parallel `GroupScheme.Gm` namespace; it's building one
124	  concrete `GrpObj` instance using Mathlib's `GrpObj`/`ofRepresentableBy` mechanism.
125	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED**. The project's encoding is the right shape. Future
126	  upstream-able as `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Gm` (single file, ~80 LOC), but the
127	  *encoding* is identical to what such an upstream file would land.
128	
129	### Decision Q2: installing `GrpObj Gm` via `ofRepresentableBy`
130	
131	- **Mathlib idiom**: `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy X F α` (`Cartesian.Grp_.lean:35`) is THE
132	  idiom for installing `GrpObj` on an object that represents a presheaf of groups.
133	  `F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat` and `α : (F ⋙ forget _).RepresentableBy X` are the two inputs.
134	  This is the canonical and ONLY idiom: the alternative "construct `μ`, `η`, `ι` by
135	  hand and prove the 5 axioms" is the explicit `GrpObj.mk` path; `ofRepresentableBy`
136	  packages it. No Mathlib code uses `GrpObj.mk` directly for a non-trivial group
137	  scheme.
138	- **Project's current path**: planned to use `ofRepresentableBy`; body is `sorry`.
139	- **The witness — concrete recipe**:
140	  - **The functor** `F : (Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{?}` is `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`.
141	    The morphism action: an `Over (Spec k̄)`-morphism `φ : T → T'` induces a ring map
142	    `Γ(T'.left, ⊤) → Γ(T.left, ⊤)` (via `φ.1.appTop`), which restricts to a group hom
143	    on units. (Use `Units.map` on the comap.)
144	  - **The representable-by witness** `α : (F ⋙ forget _).RepresentableBy (Gm kbar)`
145	    constructs `homEquiv : (T ⟶ Gm) ≃ Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. Built in three steps:
146	    1. `T ⟶ Gm` (in `Over (Spec k̄)`) ≃ `T.left ⟶ Spec (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` over `Spec k̄`
147	       (the over-category encoding).
148	    2. `T.left ⟶ Spec (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` over `Spec k̄` ≃ `k̄[t, t⁻¹] →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤)` as
149	       `k̄`-algebra maps (the `Spec` adjunction `ΓSpec.adjunction` restricted to the
150	       over-category). Specifically: a morphism `T.left ⟶ Spec R` over `Spec k̄` is
151	       (by `ΓSpec.adjunction.homEquiv`) a ring map `R →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤)`; the "over
152	       `Spec k̄`" condition makes it a `k̄`-algebra map.
153	    3. `k̄[t, t⁻¹] →ₐ[k̄] Γ(T.left, ⊤)` ≃ `{u : Γ(T.left, ⊤) // IsUnit u}`
154	       (i.e. `Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`). This is the `IsLocalization.Away.lift` API:
155	       a `k̄`-algebra map `k̄[t] →ₐ[k̄] Γ(T.left, ⊤)` is the choice of an element
156	       `u = (f X) ∈ Γ(T.left, ⊤)` (`MvPolynomial.aeval u`), and it factors through
157	       `Localization.Away X = k̄[t, t⁻¹]` iff `u` is a unit (`IsLocalization.Away.lift`
158	       gives the factorisation; `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit_iff` is the
159	       reverse direction).
160	  - **The `homEquiv_comp` field**: naturality. Reduces to checking that pre-composing
161	    `T → Gm` with a morphism `φ : T'' → T` produces the comap of the unit. This is
162	    immediate from `Spec.map`-naturality + `Spec` adjunction + `Units.map`.
163	- **Gap**: in-proposal. The path is well-defined and uses canonical Mathlib pieces; the
164	  reason iter-166 deferred was the multi-step bijection composition (one needs the
165	  over-category Spec-adjunction + Localization.Away.lift bridge, which is not a single
166	  Mathlib lemma).
167	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED with `ofRepresentableBy`**. The chain is:
168	  `(T ⟶ Gm)_{/Spec k̄}` 
169	  $\xrightarrow{\text{over-cat unfold}}$ `{f : T.left ⟶ Spec(k̄[t,t⁻¹]) // f ≫ struct = T.hom}`
170	  $\xrightarrow{\Gamma\text{Spec}}$ `{φ : k̄[t,t⁻¹] →+* Γ(T,⊤) // φ ∘ algebraMap k̄ _ = ...}`
171	  $\xrightarrow{\text{IsLocalization.Away.lift}}$ `{u : Γ(T,⊤) // IsUnit u}` = `Γ(T,⊤)ˣ`.
172	  No parallel API, no custom `GroupScheme.Hom` infrastructure. Bonus: the same recipe
173	  (with the additive functor `AddGrpCat.of Γ(T,⊤)`, using `AffineSpace.homOverEquiv`
174	  directly in step 2-3) closes `ga_grpObj` — see `gm-scaling-p1.md` D2.a.
175	
176	### Decision Q3: `gmScalingP1` chartwise glue
177	
178	- **Mathlib idiom**: `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` over a 2-chart `Proj.affineOpenCover`
179	  (specialised to the irrelevant ideal's 2 generators `X₀, X₁`), each chart's morphism
180	  given by `Spec.map` of a polynomial ring map. The cross-chart agreement is checked
181	  using `pullbackSpecIso` to identify each chart's intersection with the other as `Spec`
182	  of a ring tensor, then proving a ring-level equation.
183	- **Concrete recipe**:
184	  - Two-chart cover: `affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan 𝒜 (f := ![X₀, X₁]) ...` —
185	    explicitly the 2-element cover indexed by `Fin 2`. (`Proj.affineOpenCover` is the
186	    "all positive-degree elements" cover; we want the 2-element specialisation.)
187	    Each chart is `Spec (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 X_i)` — for the standard
188	    ℕ-graded `k̄[X₀, X₁]`, this homogeneous localization is `k̄[X_{1-i} / X_i]` (single
189	    variable in degree-0 = polynomial ring in `t = X_{1-i}/X_i`). Mathlib has the
190	    iso witness via `HomogeneousLocalization.Away.algebraMap` etc., but the
191	    project-side bridge "Spec(HomogeneousLocalization.Away X_i) ≅ Spec(k̄[t])"
192	    is needed (NOT shipped for `MvPolynomial`-specific gradings; project owes this
193	    iso, ~30 LOC).
194	  - Chart-side morphism `(σ_×)_i : Spec(k̄[t_i]) ⊗_{Spec k̄} Spec(k̄[λ, λ⁻¹]) ⟶
195	    Spec(k̄[t_i])`: use `pullbackSpecIso` to identify the product as
196	    `Spec(k̄[t_i, λ, λ⁻¹])`; then `(σ_×)_0 = Spec.map (k̄[t] →+* k̄[t, λ, λ⁻¹], t ↦ λ·t)`
197	    on chart 0 (affine), and `(σ_×)_1 = Spec.map (k̄[u] →+* k̄[u, λ, λ⁻¹], u ↦ u/λ)`
198	    on chart 1 (near ∞). Both are ring-level definitions.
199	  - Cross-chart agreement: on the intersection `Spec(k̄[t, t⁻¹]) ⊗ Spec(k̄[λ, λ⁻¹])
200	    ≅ Spec(k̄[t, t⁻¹, λ, λ⁻¹])`, both chart maps reduce to `t ↦ λ·t = (λ·t⁻¹)⁻¹`;
201	    `pullbackSpecIso` + ring-level computation closes this in `ext + simp + ring`.
202	- **Project's current path**: `gmScalingP1 := sorry` (iter-165 deferred to iter-166;
203	  iter-166 closed the consumer via sorryAx-propagation, so the body is still owed).
204	- **Gap**: in-proposal. The path is clear; the iter-166 hold-up was the
205	  Mathlib-orchestration weight (3-4 sub-lemmas worth: HomogeneousLocalization.Away iso,
206	  pullbackSpecIso bridge, polynomial ring-map definition, glue agreement).
207	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED with `glueMorphisms` + `pullbackSpecIso`**. NO custom `Cover`
208	  / `MulAction` typeclass; bare morphism only. The companion lemma
209	  `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` follows from a chart-level direct computation (on
210	  chart 0, `(0, λ) ↦ λ·0 = 0` is a `Spec.map` of a ring map sending `t ↦ 0`, which is
211	  exactly the structure map of `zeroPt = Proj.fromOfGlobalSections of (X₀ ↦ 0, X₁ ↦ 1)`).
212	
213	### Decision Q4: the four product / Proj-integrality instances
214	
215	#### Q4(a) — `GeometricallyIrreducible ((ℙ¹) ⊗ (Gm)).hom`
216	
217	- **Mathlib idiom**: composition via `GeometricallyIrreducible.comp`
218	  (`Geometrically/Irreducible.lean:121`). `(X ⊗ Y).hom = (pullback X.hom Y.hom).hom =
219	  pullback.fst X.hom Y.hom ≫ X.hom`. Both factors are GI (the `pullback.fst` from
220	  `GeometricallyIrreducible.IsStableUnderBaseChange`, the `X.hom` from the project's
221	  `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred` instance). The composition lemma requires
222	  `UniversallyOpen` on both — supplied for free by smoothness via
223	  `UniversallyOpen.of_flat` (`Morphisms/UniversallyOpen.lean:145`) + `Smooth → Flat`
224	  (`Morphisms/Smooth.lean:113`).
225	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED**. Code shape:
226	  ```lean
227	  haveI : GeometricallyIrreducible ((P¹) ⊗ (Gm)).hom :=
228	    GeometricallyIrreducible.comp (pullback.fst P¹.hom Gm.hom) P¹.hom
229	  ```
230	  (with `UniversallyOpen.of_flat` instances inferred from smoothness). Sub-prerequisite:
231	  `[GeometricallyIrreducible (Gm kbar).hom]` — currently NOT installed on `Gm`. This is
232	  the next gap; for `Spec (integral k̄-algebra)` over `Spec k̄`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`
233	  is automatic since base-change to `Spec k̄` is identity, hence reduces to
234	  `IrreducibleSpace (Spec (Localization.Away X))` which is automatic from
235	  `Localization.Away X` being a domain (localization of integral domain). The Mathlib
236	  bridge is `irreducibleSpace_of_isDomain` (or directly via
237	  `PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace_iff_isDomain`).
238	
239	#### Q4(b) — `LocallyOfFiniteType ((ℙ¹) ⊗ (Gm)).hom`
240	
241	- **Mathlib idiom**: TRIVIAL. `LocallyOfFiniteType` is `IsStableUnderComposition` AND
242	  `IsStableUnderBaseChange` (`Morphisms/FiniteType.lean:63,79`). `(X ⊗ Y).hom =
243	  pullback.fst ≫ X.hom`. Both pieces LOFT, composition LOFT. `LocallyOfFinitePresentation
244	  → LocallyOfFiniteType` is also free for both factors (and for ℙ¹ specifically: `Proj`
245	  of finite-type-over-k̄ is LOFT via `Proj.awayι_toSpecZero` + `HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff`
246	  + `RingHom.FiniteType`; not auto-instanced, but the project can install it on
247	  `ProjectiveLineBar`).
248	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED**. `infer_instance` should close it once a
249	  `[LocallyOfFiniteType (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom]` instance is installed (currently
250	  missing — derivable from `IsProper` is actually not direct; need explicit `Proj.toSpecZero`
251	  LOFT instance which is shipped by the `Proper.lean` chain at L143). The `Gm` side is
252	  free (`LocallyOfFinitePresentation → LocallyOfFiniteType` instance shipped).
253	
254	#### Q4(c) — `IsReduced ((ℙ¹) ⊗ (Gm)).left`
255	
256	- **Mathlib idiom**: The shipped lemma is `GeometricallyReduced.isReduced_of_flat_of_isLocallyNoetherian` (`Geometrically/Reduced.lean:106`):
257	  `[GeometricallyReduced f] [Flat f] [IsReduced Y] [IsLocallyNoetherian Y] →
258	  IsReduced X`, with the companion `instance` (`L121`/`L125`) giving the product
259	  version. **For the project's setting** (`Y = Spec k̄`, both factors reduced + smooth):
260	  - `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)` — free (`k̄` is Noetherian as a field).
261	  - `IsReduced (Spec k̄)` — free.
262	  - `Flat (ProjectiveLineBar).hom` — from `Smooth → Flat`.
263	  - `GeometricallyReduced (ProjectiveLineBar).hom` — **NOT free**. Mathlib does NOT
264	    ship `Smooth → GeometricallyReduced` at scheme level. Standard math fact (smooth
265	    morphisms are geometrically regular hence geometrically reduced), but the bridge
266	    `Smooth ⟹ Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced ⟹ AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyReduced`
267	    is missing.
268	- **Gap**: GAP_EXISTS — Mathlib gap, not project gap. The project should either:
269	  - (i) install a one-off project-side instance `Smooth f → GeometricallyReduced f`
270	    (~30 LOC, scheme-level extension of `Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced`), or
271	  - (ii) bypass `GeometricallyReduced` entirely by noting that `(ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm)` is
272	    explicitly the pullback `Proj 𝒜 ×_{Spec k̄} Spec(k̄[t, t⁻¹])`, locally affine, and
273	    reduce to a tensor-product-of-domains argument on each affine chart of `ℙ¹`. Each
274	    chart is `Spec (k̄[t]) ⊗_{Spec k̄} Spec(k̄[λ, λ⁻¹]) = Spec(k̄[t, λ, λ⁻¹])`, which is a
275	    domain over k̄ (algebraically closed), hence reduced; `IsReduced.of_openCover`
276	    closes (`Geometrically/Reduced.lean:?` shipped).
277	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED via path (ii)** (chart-local), NOT path (i). Reason:
278	  - The "install `Smooth → GeometricallyReduced`" instance is genuine Mathlib
279	    infrastructure work and may be 100+ LOC; out of scope for an iter-167 prover lane.
280	  - The chart-local path uses ONLY `IsReduced.of_openCover` + the fact that
281	    `Spec(domain over k̄)` is reduced — both routine. The hard part (which 2 charts)
282	    is already done by `Proj.affineOpenCover`, and the same pullbackSpecIso bridge
283	    from Q3 supplies the chart description.
284	  - **Bonus**: this same chart-local technique handles Q4(d), so the project's
285	    reduction is double-leveraged. Project owes ~40 LOC for one helper lemma
286	    `isReduced_of_affineCover_isDomain` (or `IsReduced.of_isOpenCover_isDomain`)
287	    that takes a cover with each chart's global-sections-ring a domain over an
288	    integral base.
289	
290	#### Q4(d) — `IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar).left`
291	
292	- **Mathlib idiom**: NOT shipped. The standard technique is
293	  `IsReduced.of_openCover` (`AlgebraicGeometry.IsReduced.of_openCover` or via
294	  `IsReduced.of_isOpenImmersion` composed with `Cover`-decomposition). For the project's
295	  case, the open cover is `Proj.affineOpenCover 𝒜` specialised to the 2-element form
296	  `{D₊(X₀), D₊(X₁)}`. Each `D₊(X_i)` is `Spec (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 X_i)`,
297	  which is `Spec` of an integral domain (a localisation of the integral graded ring
298	  `k̄[X₀, X₁]`'s degree-0 piece in chart 0 = `k̄[X₁/X₀]`, which is a polynomial ring
299	  in one variable, hence a domain). Domain ⟹ Reduced ⟹ `IsReduced (Spec _)`.
300	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED via `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `Proj.affineOpenCover`**.
301	  Same helper as Q4(c) discharges; the missing piece is
302	  `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 X_i` is a domain for the standard ℕ-grading on
303	  `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. This is a ring-level fact: it's a localization of
304	  `k̄[X₀, X₁]` at the powers of `X_i`, restricted to degree-0. Mathlib has
305	  `HomogeneousLocalization.isDomain` for `Proj` of an integral domain (file
306	  `RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization`); the project then uses
307	  `IsReduced.of_isDomain`.

311	**For Q2 (gm_grpObj):** Use `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with the 3-step bijection chain.
312	Sketch:
313	```lean
314	instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) := by
315	  refine GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar)
316	    { obj := fun T => GrpCat.of (Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤))ˣ
317	      map := fun {T T'} φ => GrpCat.ofHom (Units.map (φ.unop.1.appTop).hom.toMonoidHom)
318	      map_id := …
319	      map_comp := … }
320	    ?_
321	  refine
322	    { homEquiv := fun {T} => ?_
323	      homEquiv_comp := ?_ }
324	  -- Build the Equiv (T ⟶ Gm) ≃ Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ via:
325	  -- 1. Over-cat unfold: (T ⟶ Gm)_{/Spec k̄} ≃ {f : T.left ⟶ Spec(k̄[t,t⁻¹]) // IsOver Spec k̄}
326	  -- 2. ΓSpec.adjunction restricted to the over-cat: ≃ k̄[t,t⁻¹] →ₐ[k̄] Γ(T.left, ⊤)
327	  -- 3. IsLocalization.Away.lift + IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit_iff:
328	  --    ≃ {u : Γ(T.left, ⊤) // IsUnit u} = Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ
329	  sorry  -- the 3-step bijection composition (carry from iter-167 lane)

910	/-- **Helper (pointed case): a morphism `f : ℙ¹ → A` killing `0 ∈ ℙ¹` is the constant `1`.**
911	Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, if `f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A` satisfies
912	`ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f = η[A]`, then `f = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A)`
913	(equivalently `f = toUnit ProjectiveLineBar ≫ η[A]`).
914	
915	This is the pointed core of the `𝔾ₘ`-scaling shortcut: form `h := gmScalingP1 ≫ f`, feed it
916	to Cor 1.5 (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) with `V = ProjectiveLineBar`, `W = Gm`, base
917	points `zeroPt`, `onePt`. The `W`-axis collapses by `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`, leaving
918	`h = fst ≫ fV` (the relation `f(λx) = fV(x)`). Specialising at `x = 1` (via the canonical
919	inclusion `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` given by `λ ↦ σ×(1, λ) = λ`) and using density of `Gm ⊆ ℙ¹` plus
920	separatedness of `A` (via `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'`, the same Mathlib bridge
921	`rigidity_core` uses inline), we conclude `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ (onePt ≫ fV)`. The basepoint
922	hypothesis then pins `onePt ≫ fV = η[A]`.
923

928	  gmScalingP1` (depends on the concrete `gmScalingP1` body, a Lane 2 scaffold).
929	These propagate `sorryAx` to the consumer; the proof structure is complete and lifts to
930	axiom-clean once the residuals discharge. -/

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Comma.Over.OverClass
10	
11	/-!
12	# Typeclasses for `S`-schemes and `S`-morphisms
13	
14	We define these as thin wrappers around `CategoryTheory/Comma/OverClass`.
15	
16	## Main definition
17	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over`: `X.Over S` equips `X` with an `S`-scheme structure.
18	  `X ↘ S : X ⟶ S` is the structure morphism.
19	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.IsOver`: `f.IsOver S` asserts that `f` is an `S`-morphism.
20	
21	-/
22	
23	@[expose] public section
24	
25	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
26	
27	universe u
28	
29	open CategoryTheory
30	
31	variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.Hom Y) (S S' : Scheme.{u})
32	
33	/--
34	`X.Over S` is the typeclass containing the data of a structure morphism `X ↘ S : X ⟶ S`.
35	-/
36	protected abbrev Over (X S : Scheme.{u}) := OverClass X S
37	
38	/--
39	`X.CanonicallyOver S` is the typeclass containing the data of a structure morphism `X ↘ S : X ⟶ S`,
40	and that `S` is (uniquely) inferable from the structure of `X`.
41	-/
42	abbrev CanonicallyOver (X S : Scheme.{u}) := CanonicallyOverClass X S
43	
44	/-- Given `X.Over S` and `Y.Over S` and `f : X ⟶ Y`,
45	`f.IsOver S` is the typeclass asserting `f` commutes with the structure morphisms. -/
46	abbrev Hom.IsOver (f : X.Hom Y) (S : Scheme.{u}) [X.Over S] [Y.Over S] := HomIsOver f S
47	
48	@[simp]
49	lemma Hom.isOver_iff [X.Over S] [Y.Over S] {f : X ⟶ Y} : f.IsOver S ↔ f ≫ Y ↘ S = X ↘ S :=
50	  ⟨fun H ↦ H.1, fun h ↦ ⟨h⟩⟩
51	
52	/-! Also note the existence of `CategoryTheory.IsOverTower X Y S`. -/
53	
54	/-- Given `X.Over S`, this is the bundled object of `Over S`. -/
55	abbrev asOver (X S : Scheme.{u}) [X.Over S] := OverClass.asOver X S
56	
57	/-- Given a morphism `X ⟶ Y` with `f.IsOver S`, this is the bundled morphism in `Over S`. -/
58	abbrev Hom.asOver (f : X.Hom Y) (S : Scheme.{u}) [X.Over S] [Y.Over S] [f.IsOver S] :=
59	  OverClass.asOverHom S f
60	
61	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
62
